info.name: Pepper Escrow Client Integration
info.description:
API collection for merchants integrating with the Pepper Escrow platform.

## Quick Start
1. **Get an API Key** — Register at `{{base_url}}/merchant/register` and get your API key from the dashboard.
2. **Set your API Key** — In Postman, edit the `api_key` variable with your key.
3. **Choose a Base URL** — Set `base_url` to your Pepper Escrow instance (e.g. `https://api.pepperescrow.com`).

## Authentication
All API endpoints use Bearer token authentication. Add this header to every request:
```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

## Environment Variables
- `base_url` — Your Pepper Escrow instance URL
- `api_key` — Your API key from the merchant dashboard
- `appid` — Your app ID (returned during registration)
- `referenceid` — A unique reference for test transactions

## Webhooks
Set your webhook URL in the merchant dashboard settings. Pepper Escrow will POST to your endpoint when:
- `payment.received` — A buyer has paid
- `transaction.released` — Funds released to you
- `settlement.completed` — Payout sent to your bank account
- `reward.earned` — Customer earned reward points

Webhook payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256 in the `X-Pepper-Signature` header.

## Support
Contact the platform operator for API keys, gateway configuration, and troubleshooting.

item.name: 1. Escrow Transactions
item.description: Core escrow operations — create, manage, and release escrow transactions.

request.description:
Creates an escrow transaction. Use this for bank transfers or manual payment tracking.
- `referenceid` must be unique per transaction
- `transaction_type` can be: escrow, deposit, withdrawal, subscription, milestone, instant
- `metadata` can hold any custom data (order_id, notes, etc.)

Creates an escrow transaction and generates a checkout URL for the buyer.
- Include `callback_url` to redirect the buyer back to your site after payment
- Response includes `payment_url` — redirect the buyer to this URL
- Commission is auto-calculated from your commission rules
Retrieve the payment URL for an existing transaction. Useful if the buyer lost the link.
Flag a transaction to prevent release. Use when there's a dispute or issue.
Release escrowed funds to the merchant. Once released, funds are available for settlement.
Request more time to fulfill the order.
Accept or reject an extension request. Set `action` to "accept" or "refuse".
Initiate a refund request.
Approve or reject a refund. `action`: "accept" (process refund) or "refuse".

query.description: Status: Open, Pending, PaymentPending, Flagged, Fulfilled, Released, Closed

request.description: Fetch all transactions filtered by status.
Get a single transaction's full details by your reference ID.
File a dispute against a transaction. Use multipart/form-data to attach files.

formdata.value: Customer did not receive the item

request.description: Returns available banks and their codes for bank transfer payments.

item.name: 4. Bank Transfer / Virtual Accounts
item.description: Accept payments via bank transfer. Each customer gets a unique virtual account number (DVA/VAF).

request.description: Assign a virtual account to a transaction. The customer pays by transferring to this account. A new DVA/VAF is created if one doesn't already exist for this customer.

item.name: 5. Wallets
item.description: Manage internal wallets for your customers. Wallets track balances within the platform.

item.name: 6. Client Configuration
item.description: Manage your account settings, bank accounts, and API token preferences.

request.description: Update your escrow and settlement preferences.
Add a bank account to receive settlement payouts. The default account is used for automatic settlements.

item.name: 7. Transaction Types & Commission Rules
item.description: Configure transaction categories and commission/fee rules.

request.description: Seeds 6 types: escrow, deposit, withdrawal, subscription, milestone, instant. Each gets a default 2.5% commission rule.
Preview what commission would be charged on a given amount.

item.name: 8. Subscriptions
item.description: Set up subscription plans and manage customer subscriptions.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Premium Plan",
  "slug": "premium",
  "description": "Monthly premium subscription",
  "amount": 50000,
  "currency": "NGN",
  "billing_frequency": "monthly",
  "billing_interval": 1,
  "trial_days": 7,
  "features": {"max_transactions": 1000, "api_access": true, "priority_support": true},
  "is_active": true
}

item.name: 9. Settlements
item.description: Withdraw your available balance. Settlements batch released transactions and initiate payouts to your bank account.

request.description: See which transactions would be included in the next settlement batch.
Process a pending settlement — sends money to your bank account via the configured gateway.

item.name: 10. Revenue Analytics
item.description: View your transaction volume and commission earnings.

request.description: Period: 7d, 30d, 90d, 1y, mtd, ytd

item.name: 11. Rewards & Listing Fees
item.description: Set up customer loyalty programs and listing/product fees.

item.name: 12. Notifications
item.description: View notification delivery logs and test your webhook endpoint.

request.description: Sends a test payload to your configured webhook URL. Check your server logs to verify.
